name: Bank Of Little Rock
description_short: Bank of Little Rock is a community bank offering FDIC-insured checking, savings, and lending products to individuals and businesses in Arkansas.
description_long: Bank of Little Rock is a local Arkansas bank serving the Little Rock area with a focus on community-based banking. The institution is FDIC-insured and emphasizes personal relationships and local reinvestment as core values. The bank highlights its commitment to knowing customers by name and supporting local entrepreneurs and nonprofits through financing and community partnerships. Founded on the premise that choosing to bank locally creates growth opportunities for the community, Bank of Little Rock positions itself as an alternative to national chains, with staff described as "friendly faces" at the teller desk. The bank operates physical branch locations in Pulaski County and Saline County. Bank of Little Rock offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services. For consumers, products include checking accounts (including Kasasa-branded accounts offering up to 6.00% APY), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, health savings accounts, and personal loans. Their Kasasa Cash Checking account is promoted as a key product with high interest rates conditional on meeting transaction requirements. The bank also provides home mortgages, home equity loans, auto loans, and debit cards (recently transitioning to Visa). For businesses, they offer business checking, business savings, money market accounts, CDs, and commercial health savings accounts, along with business lending solutions. Digital banking services, fraud protection through Kasasa Protect, and standard account services are available online. What distinguishes Bank of Little Rock is its emphasis on community integration and relationship banking. The bank explicitly markets its role in supporting local homebuyers, entrepreneurs, and nonprofit organizations. Their product lineup features Kasasa accounts prominently—these reward accounts incentivize debit card usage and offer tiered interest rates based on qualification criteria. The transition to Visa debit cards with contactless payment capability represents a modernization effort. The bank also emphasizes accessibility, with AudioEye-enabled website optimization and proactive communication about fraud prevention (phishing warnings). However, several limitations emerge from the available information. The website does not disclose specific loan terms, rates, or fees beyond the checking account APY figures. Qualification requirements for high-interest accounts are conditional (e.g., 6.00% APY requires 30 debit card transactions monthly, with 0.05% APY as fallback), making actual returns uncertain for many customers. The bank's geographic footprint appears limited to central Arkansas, restricting accessibility for out-of-area consumers. Product comparison tools are mentioned but not fully detailed on the homepage. No information is provided about credit card products, investment services, or wealth management—services commonly offered by larger regional banks.
services: FDIC-insured checking accounts (Classic, Prime, Budget, Free Kasasa Cash, Free Kasasa Cash Back, Free Kasasa Tunes variants)
Kasasa Cash Checking with up to 6.00% APY on balances meeting transaction requirements
Personal savings accounts, youth savings accounts, and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business customers
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts
Personal loans for consolidation, purchases, and other needs
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Business checking, savings, and money market accounts
Visa debit cards with contactless tap-to-pay functionality
Digital banking and online account management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government full faith and credit
Kasasa Cash Checking offers up to 6.00% APY—significantly higher than national bank averages
Kasasa Cash Back provides 1.50% cash back on debit purchases, up to $45 annually
Community-focused lending for mortgages, home equity, auto loans, and small business financing
Kasasa Saver offers 1.00% APY on balances up to $50,000 for qualifying customers
Free account switching tools and migration support for customers from other banks
Fraud protection through Kasasa Protect comprehensive security solution
Online account opening capability allowing customers to start banking within minutes
cons: High-yield APY rates are conditional on meeting transaction minimums (30 debit card transactions/month), with substantial rate drops (to 0.05% APY) if not met
Limited geographic service area restricted to Pulaski County and Saline County in Arkansas, making in-person banking inaccessible for out-of-area customers
No credit card products mentioned, limiting rewards and revolving credit options compared to full-service banks
Website lacks specific loan terms, APRs, fees, and qualification criteria for mortgages, auto loans, and personal loans
Kasasa account rates appear promotional/introductory without transparency on sustainability or long-term rate guarantees

contact.address: 200 N State St, Little Rock, AR 72201

best_for: Arkansas residents in Little Rock, Pulaski County, or Saline County seeking high-yield checking accounts with active debit card usage
Local small business owners and entrepreneurs needing relationship-based lending and community bank support
Homebuyers in central Arkansas interested in locally-originated mortgage and home equity lending
Community-minded depositors who prioritize supporting local nonprofits and businesses through their banking choices
diagnosis: Bank of Little Rock is best for Arkansas-based consumers and small businesses seeking community-focused banking with above-market savings rates, particularly those willing to meet Kasasa account qualification requirements (30+ monthly debit transactions). The main caveat is that promotional high-yield rates (6.00% APY) are conditional and drop dramatically to 0.05% if transaction minimums aren't met, and the bank's service area is limited to central Arkansas, making it inaccessible for customers outside the region seeking in-person banking relationships.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 200 N State St, Little Rock, AR 72201
